Patch Set 18: Code-Review-1

i don't sure it's usefull patch due hide a large number of bugs.
As example - last short read i see have a result a bug in with LDLM processing described in LU-5569.

I think all errors should be handled on low level if need.

Patch Set 18:

Don't get what do you mean, how could this hide a large number of bugs?

Patch Set 18:

Shadow, I think the scope of this restart is narrow enough to be ok.

Basically the restart is only happening if ci_need_restart is set, which only happens if the layout was somehow invalidated (could also happen unintentionally unfortunately due to bundled layout lock revocation).

Patch Set 18:

(4 comments)

Shadow, I think the latest version of this patch is better than older versions and should avoid the problem you raise about restarting for arbitrary error conditions.  It is only restarting in the case the layout was actually changed.


One question that I have about this patch, which might be answered by a better commit comment and/or comments in the code, is what happens in the original case of restarting the IO because the lock was cancelled due to shared IBITS locks?  The point of the check in can_populate_pages() was to restart the IO from the beginning if the objects migrated to new OSTs so that the in-flight IO was not lost (it is the responsibility of the migrator to copy the rest of the file data).  It looks like this case is still OK (vvp_io_init() and vvp_io_fini() are still checking if the layout generation actually changed.

What seems to be missing (or maybe I just don't see it) is restarting the IO when the LAYOUT lock bit is cancelled _without_ the layout actually changing (which I think was the original cause of LU-6389).  In that case, the client will re-fetch the layout lock, maybe again combined with UPDATE, or maybe separate.  I don't think io->ci_need_restart will be set (nor should it, because the whole IO doesn't need to be restarted, only a continuation of the current IO).  How does this patch address this case?

Patch Set 18:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 18:

(2 comments)

Patch Set 18:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 18:

Hi Shadow,

Does Bobijam, Oleg, and Andreas answer your questions? Please respond.

commit.subject: LU-6389 llite: restart short read/write for normal IO
commit.message:
LU-6389 llite: restart short read/write for normal IO

If normal IO got short read/write, we'd restart the IO from where
we've accomplished until we meet EOF or error happens.
